verdict_reasoning: APOB is a secreted apolipoprotein synthesized in liver and intestine, incorporated into circulating lipoproteins (VLDL, LDL, chylomicrons), and released into the bloodstream. It lacks a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or other membrane-anchoring mechanism. While present in the extracellular milieu, it does not stably associate with the cell surface; it circulates freely in plasma as a component of lipoprotein particles. Washing cells would remove APOB, indicating no stable physical link to the cell membrane.
